failure_surface: Evaluation test case returned 0 (failure) despite successful code execution printing 'Successfully transposed 7 records'. Output file content did not match expected criteria.
root_cause: The agent attempted to load an existing output file if it existed, potentially leaving stale data from previous runs that contaminated the output. The code also lacked validation to ensure only the target range (D1:F7) contained data, and did not guarantee a clean workspace for Cell-Level Manipulation tasks.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: For Cell-Level Manipulation tasks generating new output files, always create a fresh workbook instead of loading an existing output file. Clear any pre-existing content on the target worksheet to prevent data contamination. Verify output dimensions match expected ranges after writing.
evidence: Code includes try/except block that loads existing output_path if it exists: 'if file exists, load it; otherwise create new workbook'
No cleanup logic exists to clear cells outside the target D1:F7 range on the output sheet
No validation step confirms output file contains only the expected data dimensions before saving
Evaluation result shows test_case_results: [0] indicating output mismatch despite successful print statements
SKILL.md states 'Verify the output matches expected dimensions after manipulation' but agent did not implement this validation

memory_items.title: Fresh Workbook for New Outputs
memory_items.lesson: When generating a new output file for Cell-Level Manipulation, always create a fresh workbook rather than loading an existing file at the output path.
memory_items.generalization: Prevents stale data contamination from previous runs and ensures clean workspace for testing.

memory_items.title: Validate Output Dimensions
memory_items.lesson: After writing data, verify that only the target cell range contains data and row/column counts match expected dimensions.
memory_items.generalization: Ensures output integrity and catches cases where extra rows or columns were inadvertently created or retained.

memory_items.title: Target Range Isolation
memory_items.lesson: For Cell-Level Manipulation tasks, explicitly isolate and control only the specified cell range, leaving surrounding cells empty or unchanged as appropriate.
memory_items.generalization: Maintains precision required for targeted cell operations and prevents unintended data spread.

edits.content:
## 6. Output Hygiene & Validation
- **New Outputs**: For Cell-Level Manipulation tasks creating output files, always instantiate a fresh workbook (not load an existing file at the output path) to prevent stale data contamination.
- **Dimension Verification**: After writing data, validate that only the target cell range contains data and row/column counts match expected dimensions.
- **Range Isolation**: Ensure operations affect only the specified cell range; surrounding cells should remain empty or unchanged per task requirements.
- **Content Cleanup**: Before writing to an existing output workbook, clear the target worksheet to remove any prior session data.
